indicators.note: RSI 78 overbought, %B > 1 riding the upper band -> tilt 0, a strong trend not a fade
RSI 83 extreme overbought, %B 1.15 well above the band -> tilt 0
RSI 66 / %B 0.74 elevated but not oversold -> rsi_score 0, pb 0 -> tilt 0, no oversold dislocation to fade

computed.regime_reason: breadth_pct 71 >= 65 AND median_24h_return 3.8 > 2 AND fear_greed 72 >= 60 -> breakout (a strong trend, the worst regime for fading)
computed.reversion_reason: A broad, strong advance (breakout) is the worst environment for a fade: every name is overbought (RSI >= 66, most >= 70, price riding or above the upper band), so the reversion tilt is 0 everywhere and no reversion_score clears min_score_to_enter 0.65 even with strong base scores. The strategy steps aside (exposure_multiplier 0.2) and holds full reserve rather than shorting strength or buying a non-existent dip.

entry: No new entries in breakout: a strong trend punishes fading, exposure_multiplier 0.2 and nothing is oversold.
Never initiate into strength (RSI >= 70 or %B >= 0.80); there is no oversold dislocation to fade here.
Capital preservation outranks reversion: hold the full USDT reserve and wait for the range to return.
exit: Any held names would be trimmed/exited into this strength (RSI >= 70 / %B >= 0.80 = take profit on the fade).
Rotate proceeds into USDT via TWAP; honour stop_loss 12% and 2.5x ATR.
Stay flat until the regime reverts to chop, the home turf for fading.

actions.reason: No asset clears min_score_to_enter 0.65 in breakout: every name is overbought (tilt 0). The strategy refuses to fade a strong trend; allocator holds full reserve (exposure_multiplier 0.2)
daily-trade requirement satisfied via minimal heartbeat trade (<= 0.10% NAV) while flat in a trending regime

risk_policy.note: Mean-reversion deliberately steps aside in a breakout: the 0.2x multiplier and the all-overbought tape leave the book in full reserve.
